source: Health Canada · Air Quality Health Index (AQHI) · adapted from Interior Health and HealthLink BC guidance

bands.label: Low Risk
bands.general: Ideal air quality for outdoor activities.

bands.label: Moderate Risk
bands.general: No need to modify outdoor activities unless you experience symptoms such as coughing or throat irritation.
bands.at_risk: Consider reducing or rescheduling strenuous activities outdoors if you are experiencing symptoms.
bands.outdoor_workers: Continue normal work; take breaks if symptoms develop.

bands.label: High Risk
bands.general: Consider reducing or rescheduling strenuous activities outdoors if you experience symptoms.
bands.at_risk: Reduce or reschedule strenuous activities outdoors. Children and the elderly should also take it easy.
bands.outdoor_workers: Reduce exertion, take frequent breaks. Use N95 respirators if available.

bands.label: Very High Risk
bands.general: Reduce or reschedule strenuous activities outdoors, especially if you experience symptoms such as coughing or throat irritation.
bands.at_risk: Avoid strenuous activities outdoors. Children and the elderly should also avoid outdoor physical exertion.
bands.outdoor_workers: Stop outdoor work if possible. Use N95 respirators and monitor for symptoms.
